---
title: "GoodWorkflows Synthetic Tabulation Walkthrough"
output: html_document
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)

repo_dir <- dirname(normalizePath(knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE), winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE))
fixture_dir <- file.path(repo_dir, "tests", "fixtures", "synthetic_trial_data")
plot_dir <- file.path(repo_dir, "docs", "assets", "generated")

stopifnot(dir.exists(fixture_dir))
stopifnot(file.exists(file.path(fixture_dir, "sample_metadata.csv")))
stopifnot(file.exists(file.path(fixture_dir, "subjectTable_TB.csv")))
stopifnot(dir.exists(file.path(fixture_dir, "sample_counts")))

read_matrix_market <- function(path) {
  lines <- readLines(path, warn = FALSE)
  data_lines <- lines[!grepl("^%", lines)]
  dims <- scan(text = data_lines[1], quiet = TRUE)

  matrix_data <- matrix(0L, nrow = dims[1], ncol = dims[2])

  if (length(data_lines) > 1) {
    entries <- read.table(
      text = paste(data_lines[-1], collapse = "\n"),
      col.names = c("row", "col", "value")
    )
    matrix_data[cbind(entries$row, entries$col)] <- entries$value
  }

  matrix_data
}

metadata <- read.csv(file.path(fixture_dir, "sample_metadata.csv"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
subject_table <- read.csv(file.path(fixture_dir, "subjectTable_TB.csv"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
counts_matrix <- read_matrix_market(file.path(fixture_dir, "sample_counts", "matrix.mtx"))
features <- read.delim(file.path(fixture_dir, "sample_counts", "features.tsv"), header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)[, 1]
barcodes <- read.delim(file.path(fixture_dir, "sample_counts", "barcodes.tsv"), header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)[, 1]
```

# Purpose

This notebook is the repository-local companion to the MkDocs vignette. It uses only seeded synthetic data, so it is safe to render on any machine and does not require access to Prime-seq, LabKey, or sensitive trial tables.

The fixture bundle is generated by:

```bash
Rscript tests/fixtures/simulate_trial_data.R \
  --output-dir tests/fixtures/synthetic_trial_data \
  --target all \
  --seed 20260414
```

# Workflow mapping

- `ingest_tabulate` consumes metadata-like rows and emits `subjectIdTable.csv`.
- `ingest_export` emits a 10x-like count directory with `matrix.mtx`, `features.tsv`, `barcodes.tsv`, and `obs_meta.csv`.
- The same synthetic fixture bundle is used in smoke tests and documentation examples.

# Metadata preview

```{r}
metadata_preview <- metadata[, c(
  "barcode",
  "cDNA_ID",
  "Timepoint",
  "Tissue",
  "RIRA_Immune.cellclass",
  "RIRA_TNK_v2.cellclass",
  "RIRA_Myeloid_v3.cellclass",
  "TandNK_ActivationCore_UCell"
)]

head(metadata_preview, 10)
```

```{r}
aggregate(
  barcode ~ cDNA_ID + RIRA_Immune.cellclass,
  data = metadata,
  FUN = length
)
```

# Subject-level tabulation preview

```{r}
subject_table
```

The synthetic subject table retains a small set of cohort-level fractions so downstream notebooks can verify wide-format joins and plotting logic without any live data dependency.

# Representative plots

## Metadata composition

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Synthetic immune-class composition per sample."}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(plot_dir, "synthetic-immune-composition.png"))
```

## Subject-level heatmap

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Synthetic subject-level fraction summary."}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(plot_dir, "synthetic-subject-table-heatmap.png"))
```

## Exported count matrix

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Synthetic exported count matrix."}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(plot_dir, "synthetic-count-matrix-heatmap.png"))
```

# Count export sanity check

```{r}
dim(counts_matrix)
```

```{r}
rownames(counts_matrix) <- features
colnames(counts_matrix) <- barcodes
counts_matrix
```

This is the same layout downstream readers expect from `outputs/counts/<sample>/<sample>_counts/`.

# Docs refresh commands

```bash
uvx --with matplotlib python scripts/docs/generate_example_plots.py
bash scripts/docs/generate_api_docs.sh
mkdocs build --strict
```

The published MkDocs version of this walkthrough lives at `docs/vignettes/synthetic-tabulation.md`.
